behaviors.description: All direct and transitive dependencies are extracted from the manifest and included in the vulnerability check.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at module handoff (manifest parser + dependency graph resolver + scanner input), not one isolated function. REAL-INFRA: no real network/OS primitive beyond normal local file reading is required. MOCK-HIDE: Unit would miss bugs where direct deps parse correctly but transitive deps are dropped when resolver output is merged. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: above Unit, concrete miss is incorrect merge/dedupe between parser and transitive resolver modules. Above Integration not required: behavior text does not require real subprocess/package-manager invocation or external API.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner dependency-loading path against fixture manifests/lockfiles containing direct and nested transitive deps; assert vulnerability-check input contains complete expected set (name+version) with no dropped transitive entries.

behaviors.description: The vulnerability database query returns results that match the exact version ranges in the manifest, not just the package name.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: correctness depends on real HTTP/API contract for version-range semantics. MOCK-HIDE: mocked API can trivially echo expected matches and hide real range-interpretation or encoding mismatches. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: above Unit, miss is malformed range query serialization despite correct local parsing. Above Integration, miss is real API-side behavior (range grammar/pagination/filter semantics) that stubs do not reproduce.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real (or staging) vulnerability API endpoint, query dependencies with overlapping package names but different version ranges; assert returned CVEs match only requested ranges and exclude out-of-range advisories.

behaviors.description: The severity sorting places Critical above High above Medium above Low, with CVEs of the same severity sorted by CVSS score.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior is pure ordering logic on in-memory data. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none apply. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: no concrete module-boundary failure is required to trigger this bug; a single comparator/sort function test can catch it.
behaviors.test_description: Feed unsorted CVE objects with mixed severities and CVSS values into sorting function; assert exact order (severity rank first, CVSS descending within severity).

behaviors.description: The manifest modification changes only the affected dependency's version, leaving all other entries unchanged.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears when parser/editor/writer modules are wired (round-trip mutation scope), not just a single setter function. REAL-INFRA: no real external API/subprocess required. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: above Unit, miss is writer reordering or rewriting unrelated entries during serialization even though patch function targets one key. REJECTION-GATE for System: behavior text requires content correctness, not real OS/network behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run manifest update flow on realistic manifest fixtures; assert only targeted dependency version changed and byte-level/structural equality for all untouched entries (including lockfile or adjacent sections, if applicable).

behaviors.description: The auto-generated pull request is created on the real repository with the correct single-dependency diff.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: explicitly requires real repository + real PR creation (git + hosting API). MOCK-HIDE: mocked SCM APIs can pass while auth/branch/base/diff generation fails in real environment. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: above Unit, miss is incorrect orchestration between diff builder and PR client. Above Integration, miss is real remote repo behavior (permissions/protection/PR API validation) that local stubs cannot expose.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end PR creation against a real test repository: create branch, apply dependency bump, push, open PR, then assert PR exists and diff contains exactly one dependency version change.

behaviors.description: A major version bump is correctly identified as a breaking change based on semver comparison.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior is deterministic semver comparison logic. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: no concrete integration-only failure is needed; parsing and compare rules can be validated directly with table-driven unit cases.
behaviors.test_description: Test semver comparator with version pairs (including pre-release/build metadata) and assert major bump detection is true only when semver major increases.

behaviors.description: When a breaking change is detected, PR creation is skipped and a review flag is surfaced in the scanner output.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is cross-module orchestration (semver decision -> PR service suppression -> report flag emission). REAL-INFRA: real network/repo not needed to verify skip path. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no required real OS/network/subprocess; (b) no AI output dependency; (c) no multi-agentic chain; (d) no LLM handoff. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: above Unit, miss is state handoff bug where breaking flag is set but PR client is still invoked due to wiring error. REJECTION-GATE for System: behavior text says 'skipped' and 'surfaced in output', which can be validated with in-process doubles.
behaviors.test_description: Wire scanner with real semver + reporting modules and a spy/fake PR client; trigger breaking bump and assert PR creation method was not called and output contains explicit review-required flag.
